title: Asset compilation should be disabled in production environments
description: This rule raises an issue when config.assets.compile is set to true in Rails production environment configuration files. Enabling asset compilation in production environments creates significant performance problems for Rails applications. When config.assets.compile = true is set in production, the Rails asset pipeline compiles assets on-demand during runtime.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant:
# Noncompliant: Enabling asset compilation in production
# config/environments/production.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.assets.compile = true  # Performance issue in production
  config.assets.debug = true
end
compliant:
# Compliant: Disable asset compilation in production
# config/environments/production.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.assets.compile = false
  config.assets.digest = true
  config.assets.debug = false
  
  # Precompile assets during deployment instead
  config.assets.precompile += %w[admin.js admin.css]
end

checks.message: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.
